title: Embedded PLC Audit Log Forwarding Gaps

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CR 2.8.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 CR 2.8 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.38: Embedded PLC Audit Log Forwarding Gaps.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.38' running on index=ot_eng per-controller heartbeat, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit" earliest=-14d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, controller
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by controller
| where _time>relative_time(now(),"-3d@d") AND count < baseline*0.2
| table _time, controller, count, baseline
description: Identifies PLCs that stopped emitting security audit events to Splunk compared to a seven-day baseline.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_eng and sourcetypes plc:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enable syslog/SNMP trap from PLC if supported; (2) UF on gateway concentrator; (3) alert on 24h silence; (4) spares swap procedure. Indexes required: `index=ot_eng`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=plc:audit`.
visualization: Time chart (count by PLC), Table, Single value (silent PLCs).
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `metadata` or custom heartbeat.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_eng` per-controller heartbeat.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable syslog/SNMP trap from PLC if supported; (2) UF on gateway concentrator; (3) alert on 24h silence; (4) spares swap procedure.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit" earliest=-14d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, controller
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by controller
| where _time>relative_time(now(),"-3d@d") AND count < baseline*0.2
| table _time, controller, count, baseline
```

Understanding this SPL

**Embedded PLC Audit Log Forwarding Gaps (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 2.8)** — Identifies PLCs that stopped emitting security audit events to Splunk compared to a seven-day baseline.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_eng` per-controller heartbeat. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `metadata` or custom heartbeat. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_eng; **sourcetype**: plc:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_eng, sourcetype="plc:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **_time, controller**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by controller** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where _time>relative_time(now(),"-3d@d") AND count < baseline*0.2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, baseline, controller, count.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (count by PLC), Table, Single value (silent PLCs).
grandmaExplanation: We spot PLCs that stopped emitting security audit events to Splunk compared to a seven-day baseline. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
